pass_1.bdr_summary: PropertyPulse helps property managers with 10-50 units track recurring maintenance tasks through automated scheduling and email reminders. Targets $39-79/month subscription model with pre-built task templates, photo completion tracking, and portfolio-wide maintenance calendar.

economics_snapshot.target_customer: Independent property managers with 10-50 rental units
economics_snapshot.acquisition_channel: Property management forums and local real estate investor meetups
economics_snapshot.audience_scale_guess: 5,000 independent property managers in target market

questions_for_hero: Should task completion require photo uploads or allow text-only notes?
What's the maximum number of properties per subscription tier?
Do you want calendar export functionality for external calendar apps?

pass_2.approved_bdr: PropertyPulse is a maintenance scheduling SaaS for property managers with 10-50 units. Core features include property portfolio management, recurring task templates, automated email reminders, and photo-enabled task completion tracking. Revenue model is $39-79/month subscriptions with Stripe billing.
pass_2.hld_document: System Architecture: FastAPI backend handles user auth, property CRUD, task scheduling engine, and email job queue. PostgreSQL stores users, properties, task templates, scheduled instances, and completion records. React frontend provides property dashboard, task calendar view, and completion forms. Stripe webhook integration manages subscription lifecycle. SendGrid processes reminder emails via background jobs. S3 stores task completion photos with signed URL access. Key workflows: 1) Property manager adds properties and selects maintenance templates 2) System generates recurring task instances based on frequency rules 3) Email jobs send reminders at configured intervals 4) Manager marks tasks complete with notes and photos 5) Dashboard shows upcoming and overdue tasks across portfolio.
engineering_questions: How should we handle timezone differences for properties in different locations?
What's the retry logic for failed email deliveries?
Should we implement soft deletes for properties and tasks?

pass_3.qa_hlt_document: QA Test Plan: 1) User Registration/Login - Test email validation, password requirements, session management 2) Property Management - Add/edit/delete properties, validate address formats, test property limits per subscription 3) Task Template System - Verify pre-loaded templates, custom task creation, frequency rule validation 4) Recurring Schedule Engine - Test monthly/quarterly/annual recurrence, edge cases like Feb 29, daylight savings transitions 5) Email Reminder System - Verify 7-day advance, due date, and 3-day overdue emails, test spam compliance 6) Task Completion - Photo upload limits, note character limits, completion timestamp accuracy 7) Subscription Billing - Stripe webhook handling, plan upgrades/downgrades, failed payment scenarios 8) Dashboard Performance - Load testing with 50 properties and 500+ scheduled tasks

test_vectors.description: Property manager with 25 properties schedules quarterly HVAC maintenance across all units
Annual fire safety inspection due date falls on weekend, verify email timing
Task marked complete with 3 photos and notes, verify S3 storage and retrieval

risk_register.description: Recurring task generation fails for leap year edge cases
Email delivery rate drops below 95% due to spam filtering
Photo storage costs exceed budget with high-volume users

qa_questions_for_engineering: What's the maximum photo file size and format restrictions?
How do we handle email bounces and unsubscribe requests?
Should overdue tasks have escalating reminder frequency?

combined_task_list.description: Set up FastAPI project structure with PostgreSQL connection
Implement user authentication and registration endpoints
Build property CRUD operations and database models
Create maintenance task template system and seeded data
Build email reminder system with SendGrid integration
Implement task completion with photo upload to S3
Set up Stripe subscription billing and webhooks
Create task calendar and completion interface
Test recurring task edge cases and email delivery
Beta testing with 3 property managers for 2 weeks
Day 2 check-in on technical architecture decisions
Day 15 review of property management workflow
Day 30 demo of task scheduling engine
Day 45 review of email system and billing integration
Day 60 beta testing results and launch readiness

pass_5.proposal_document_for_hero: PropertyPulse Tier 2 MVP Proposal: 75-day build timeline for full-featured maintenance scheduling SaaS. Includes user authentication, property portfolio management, recurring task engine with 30+ pre-built templates, automated email reminders via SendGrid, photo-enabled task completion, and Stripe subscription billing. Target market: independent property managers with 10-50 units at $39-79/month pricing. Key differentiators: dead simple setup, maintenance-focused (not full property management), and affordable for small operators. Technical stack: FastAPI + React + PostgreSQL deployed on Railway/Vercel. Beta testing with 3 property managers in final 15 days. Revenue goal: $50K ARR within 12 months. This expands the Tier 1 proof-of-concept with real integrations, subscription billing, automated workflows, and production-ready architecture.

hero_questions: Can we add a mobile-responsive design optimization for property managers who work on-site?
Should we include a simple reporting dashboard showing maintenance completion rates by property?
What's the plan for handling customer support during the first 90 days post-launch?
